protocol.name: tawc_buffer_v1

description.summary: AHardwareBuffer sharing for Android Wayland compositor

protocol.description: This protocol allows Wayland clients to share GPU buffers with the compositor using Android's AHardwareBuffer mechanism. Buffer data is transferred over a side-channel Unix socket (because AHB serialization uses multiple fds that don't fit Wayland's single-fd-per-argument model), while buffer lifecycle is coordinated via this protocol.

interface.name: tawc_buffer_manager_v1
interface.description: The global entry point for AHB buffer sharing. Clients bind this to create per-surface AHB channels.

request.name: get_channel

description.summary: create an AHB channel for a surface

request.description:
Creates an AHB channel associated with a wl_surface. The compositor will send a channel_fd event on the returned object with a Unix socket fd for AHB transfer.
Only one channel may be active per surface.

interface.name: tawc_ahb_channel_v1

description.summary: AHB transfer channel for a surface

interface.description:
Represents a side-channel connection for transferring AHardwareBuffers between client and compositor for a specific wl_surface.
The client sends AHBs via AHardwareBuffer_sendHandleToUnixSocket on the side-channel socket, then calls attach to inform the compositor. The compositor receives the AHB and imports it as a GPU texture.
The standard wl_surface.commit triggers presentation of the most recently attached AHB.

event.name: channel_fd
event.description: Sent by the compositor immediately after channel creation. The fd is one end of a Unix socketpair. The client uses AHardwareBuffer_sendHandleToUnixSocket on this fd to transfer AHB handles to the compositor.

request.name: attach

description.summary: attach an AHB that was sent on the side channel

request.description:
Informs the compositor that an AHardwareBuffer has been sent on the side-channel socket. The compositor will receive it via AHardwareBuffer_recvHandleFromUnixSocket.
The width and height describe the buffer dimensions. The compositor uses these for layout before the AHB is fully received.
This request is double-buffered like wl_surface.attach: it sets the pending buffer, which is applied on the next wl_surface.commit.

event.name: release

description.summary: compositor is done with the buffer

event.description: Sent when the compositor no longer needs the most recently committed AHB. After receiving this event, the client may reuse or destroy the AHardwareBuffer.

request.name: destroy
request.description: Destroys the AHB channel. Any pending or committed buffers may still be in use by the compositor until released.
